rationale: The cited paper appears highly relevant to the citing paper’s core framing: the citing study explicitly examines postpurchase evaluation and a prepurchase vs. postpurchase boundary condition, while the cited study’s main contribution is exactly the before-use vs. after-use shift in utility (feature fatigue) and post-use satisfaction. This construct-level overlap suggests the citation is plausibly theoretical/foundational rather than an incidental mention, so it warrants full relation judgment despite only DOI-level matched reference evidence.
